status: LOCKED LSTM results (zero vs semantic padding)
claim_under_test: paper §3.4.2 — semantic padding improves the LSTM
protocol: 5-fold StratifiedKFold(shuffle=True, random_state=42); macro-F1=mean(F1_pos,F1_neg); paired t + Wilcoxon on per-fold deltas; identical fold split/init/minibatch order/dropout per condition; epochs=10, max_len=300, lr=0.0005, grad_clip=5.0, collapse-retry=1

readouts.meanpool: post-pad; masked mean-pool (zero over real tokens, semantic over all slots)
readouts.prelast: pre-pad (real tokens last); last-time-step readout; semantic pads consumed sequentially before real tokens

labels_note: labels reconstructed deterministically: movie=NLTK movie_reviews pos(1000)+neg(1000); twitter=NLTK twitter_samples pos(5000)+neg(5000), emoticon distant

interpretation.headline: Paper's core deep-learning claim does NOT reproduce under fair evaluation. No cell shows a robust significant gain from semantic padding.
interpretation.decisive_cell: prelast/twitter (short-text regime, semantic's most favorable legitimate readout): Δ=+0.0032, t_p=0.214 — essentially zero; both conditions train cleanly (~0.72-0.73).
interpretation.only_nominal_positive: meanpool/movie Δ=+0.014 (t_p=0.037) is tiny, an artifact of long docs having no pad slots (semantic≡zero; fold1 Δ=0.0000), and not robust (reverses on meanpool/twitter, vanishes on prelast/movie).
interpretation.meanpool_short_text_loss: meanpool/twitter Δ=-0.226 (t_p=0.026): semantic_padding fills ~285 of 300 slots from the same alphabetical global lexicon list for every tweet; mean-pooling makes ~93% of the representation a document-independent constant, drowning real signal (features.py:106-107).
interpretation.retired_claim: 'Semantic padding improves the LSTM (~+0.20 short text)' is NOT supported: it required a last-time-step readout that collapses the zero baseline to one class (macro-F1 0.3333 on 5/5 twitter folds). Readout artifact, not a representational gain.
interpretation.robust_to_readout: The null holds under BOTH readout families (meanpool: null-to-negative; prelast: null).
interpretation.relation_to_classical: Mirrors the classical correction: a naive design inflates an effect that principled evaluation erases. For the LSTM path the honest result is a null.
